description_long: This object consists of two mushrooms with thick, beige stems and large, rounded caps. The caps are a vivid red color, adorned with irregular white spots, reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria. The stems are slightly curved and textured, with darker smudges and a somewhat bulbous base. The larger mushroom's cap is broad and flat, while the smaller one is more dome-shaped. The surface of the caps appears matte with a subtle roughness, and the stems have a fibrous, organic look. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the mushrooms are depicted as if growing from an undefined, neutral substrate.
description: Two red-capped, white-spotted mushrooms with thick, beige stems and a natural, organic texture. The larger cap is broad and flat, while the smaller is dome-shaped.
description_view_0: Front view showing both mushrooms clearly, highlighting the red caps with white spots and the full length of the stems.
description_view_1: Angled view emphasizing the curvature of the larger stem and the rounded shape of the smaller mushroom's cap.
description_view_2: Side view with both mushrooms visible, showing the broadness of the larger cap and the relative size difference.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the larger cap, with the smaller mushroom partially obscured.
